data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client served through the production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding; findings list EMPTY.

Setup facts grounding the walk: fe57 is the FOUNDATION story (declares no depends_on; "the foundation every other story builds on"). Three siblings declare depends_on fe57 and CONSUME its artifacts: 8937 (round-trip contract test), 59ef (verified-fake fidelity harness), 822a (hermetic bidirectional round-trip). fe57 file_impact = tests/fixtures/jira/ (new), the FakeAcliClient module (new), scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py (new), a fixture secret-scan test (new), and a re-capture doc — all net-new and disjoint from every sibling's edits.

7-MODE WALK (parent × fe57):
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. fe57 PRODUCES the fixtures + FakeAcliClient; 8937/59ef/822a CONSUME (read) them over declared depends_on fe57 edges. No sibling co-writes the fixtures dir or the fake module; clean producer/consumer split, no shared mutable write. Disjoint from the outbound-applier cluster (33d0/2359 → apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py) and from 8937's schema/fetcher.py edits and 59ef's contract-test/acli_graph read.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("FakeAcliClient serves fixtures through the production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation") is mutually reinforcing with 59ef AC2 (the fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client) and with 8937's snapshot-entry schema (which targets exactly those unmassaged comment/issuelinks/parent shapes). fe57 covers only the INBOUND fetch/enrichment surface; it neither asserts nor depends on the outbound labels_applied representation that is the epic's one real cross-child seam (correctly localized to the 33d0↔2359 outbound-telemetry pairing). No representation contradiction.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. fe57 is the root foundation needing no internal input, so it correctly declares no depends_on; its only external dependency (live Jira for capture) is correctly live-gated per AC3. Its declared OUT items (the contract test → story B/8937; the verified-fake honesty check → story C/59ef) are present as named siblings. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. fe57 owns fixtures + fake + capture/scrub script + re-capture doc and explicitly scopes OUT the consuming contract test (→8937) and the verified-fake fidelity harness (→59ef). 59ef VERIFIES the fake fe57 BUILDS; 8937 CONSUMES the fixtures fe57 CAPTURES; 822a reuses them for the round-trip — complementary, not duplicative. Distinct surface from every sibling (fixtures/fake vs schema vs telemetry vs applier-collapse vs probe).
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only hard ordering edge (foundation before its consumers) is captured by the three declared depends_on fe57 edges, consistent with the epic's "A → B first" thin-vertical-slice sequencing. fe57's files are disjoint from the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster, so it is not entangled in that hazard.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. fe57's deliverables are additive test scaffolding. AC4 (fixtures consumed ONLY via the production code path; no test hand-reshapes) and the documented re-capture cadence ("so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors") support the epic's anti-change-detector posture / parent "no net increase in brittle tests" rather than imposing brittle expectations. The fake adds NO shape massaging, so consumers receive exactly the production-fetcher shapes — this ENABLES rather than constrains the siblings. Secret-scrubbing (AC1/AC3 + the committed fixture secret-scan test) keeps committed fixtures safe for every consumer.
- Residual references — BENIGN. The four enrichment-method signatures the FakeAcliClient mirrors (search_issues, get_comment_map, get_issuelinks_map, get_parent_map) all exist on the real client and are routed through the production fetch_snapshot enrichment merge. 33d0's collapse/deletion targets the DEAD OUTBOUND typed-leaf applier, NOT the INBOUND enrichment surface fe57 fakes, so its deletion strands nothing fe57 references. No dangling reference.

NOTE (benign, not a finding): the plan text places the FakeAcliClient at tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py and the consuming siblings (8937/59ef/822a) reference that path; the as-shipped fake relocated into the jira_contract/ subdirectory with same-directory imports from its co-located consumers. This is an implementation-detail relocation that left no dangling reference at the plan level — the declared depends_on fe57 edges and the producer/consumer relationship are unchanged — so it reads benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter and is not a G4 cross-child inconsistency.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion is a G3 COVERAGE matter (and per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause is owned by 8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency; fe57's own re-capture doc is self-scoped to its fixtures, consistent with that. Not addressed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × fe57): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. Findings list EMPTY.
